article_title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
obligation_text: High-risk AI systems shall be accompanied by instructions for use in an appropriate digital format or otherwise that include concise, complete, correct and clear information that is relevant, accessible and comprehensible to deployers.
scoring_text: Skill in creating AI system usage documentation that meets regulatory standards for completeness, correctness, clarity, relevance, accessibility, and comprehensibility for deployer audiences.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in creating AI system usage documentation that meets regulatory standards for completeness, correctness, clarity, relevance, accessibility, and comprehensibility for deployer audiences.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in developing AI system documentation including model cards, system cards, and datasheets for datasets that meet both internal governance review and external regulatory conformity assessment requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 13(3) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.731, cosine: 0.746). Secondary: RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.727), RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.718).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.3/10 (raw 0.731). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documentation, regulatory, skill, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [accessibility, audiences, clarity, completeness, comprehensibility, correctness] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.881, R=0.879). Balanced P/R — the 22-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.746 ≈ STS 0.731 (gap 0.015). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, documentation, regulatory, skill, system] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 25.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, documentation, regulatory, skill, system]. EU AI Act-only: [accessibility, audiences, clarity, completeness, comprehensibility, correctness]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing].
